mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25% signals an accommodative monetary policy that reduces the risk-free discount rate and boosts equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate greater than 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances where UNRATE has risen more than 0.5pp over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based consumer and residential demand momentum that lifts corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months when both RSAFS and HOUST are expanding.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held below 0.25% and consumer price inflation runs below 2%, the real policy rate is deeply negative, forcing capital out of fixed income and into equities and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with below-target inflation
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of weeks when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation is below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production signals manufacturing sector recovery and rising capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate operating leverage and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during months when INDPRO is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts below one million units signals a contraction in residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and durable goods, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when HOUST is below 1000.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding household income and stronger near-term consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of weeks when PAYEMS is increasing month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Joint expansion in industrial production and housing starts signals synchronized recovery in manufacturing and construction, eliminating idiosyncratic sector risk and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months when both INDPRO and HOUST are expanding.
